events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need produce JSON answer. Need parse scenario. We are assessing suspension & steering production floor. Condition: cotter pin omitted on castle nut. Work rough-complete. Need estimate/interpret measurable condition and state why out of tolerance. Decision must be fail if dangerous or non-compliant. Cotter pin omitted on castle nut is a safety-critical fastening issue; castle nut can back off, leading to separation of steering/suspension components. So fail. Risk high or critical? In production floor, omitted cotter pin on suspension/steering castle nut is critical because could cause loss of steering control. But maybe high. Need decide. The categories: installed-defective, non-compliant. Percent complete rough-complete maybe 75. Value? Not a numeric reading; could set 0. Confidence high 0.95. Findings: cotter pin missing from castle nut; castle nut not secured against loosening. Actions: install correct cotter pin per OEM torque/alignment spec, inspect adjacent fasteners, re-verify torque. Rationale. References: OEM assembly procedures, alignment specs, maybe SAE/ISO but only OEM and alignment specs provided. Need output exactly JSON, no prose. Ensure valid JSON.

events.label: response

{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "critical",

  "percent_complete": 75,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",

  "event_time": 0,
  "rate": 0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Cotter pin is omitted from the castle nut.",
    "Castle nut is not mechanically locked and can loosen under vibration or load.",
    "Fastener does not meet OEM/assembly retention requirements for suspension/steering."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Install the correct size and type cotter pin through the castle nut and cotter hole.",
    "Verify castle nut torque to OEM specification before installing cotter pin.",
    "Inspect adjacent suspension/steering fasteners for missing cotter pins or locking devices.",
    "Re-inspect and sign off at the quality gate before closing out the work."
  ],
  "rationale": "A castle nut on a suspension or steering joint must be secured with a cotter pin to prevent the nut from backing off. Omitting the cotter pin leaves the critical joint unsecured, creating a direct risk of component separation and loss of steering control, so the assembly is out of tolerance and must be failed.",
  "references": [
    "OEM assembly procedures",
    "alignment specs"
  ]
}
